reasoning: The Lean statement correctly captures every aspect of the informal claim. S is a set in EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2) (the Euclidean plane), and convexity with 0 ∈ S is correctly asserted. The topological alternative "0 ∈ interior S ∨ IsClosed S" matches "either the origin is an interior point or S is closed". The ray condition "∀ P ≠ 0, ∃ Q, SameRay ℝ P Q ∧ Q ∉ S" correctly encodes "every ray from origin has a point outside S": when P ≠ 0, SameRay ℝ P Q means Q = 0 or Q is a positive multiple of P — i.e., Q lies on the ray from the origin in direction P. Since 0 ∈ S, the witness Q must actually be a nonzero point on the ray that's outside S. The conclusion Bornology.IsBounded S matches "S is bounded".
